{"query":"Federal Reserve Eccles Building renovation federal probe Jerome Powell 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1349,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.foxbusiness.com/politics/federal-reserve-chair-powell-under-criminal-investigation-over-hq-renovation-report","title":"Fed's Powell under criminal investigation over HQ renovation | Fox Business","content":"President Donald Trump threatened to sue Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell over the central bank's costly renovation project.
"This new threat is not about my testimony last June or about the renovation of the Federal Reserve buildings," Powell said in a video statement Sunday evening.
The White House did not immediately respond to Fox News Digital's request for comment.
Powell held the benchmark rate at 4.25% to 4.5% as the Fed took a wait-and-see approach to assess the impact of Trump’s sweeping tariffs.
"We decided to take it on because, honestly, when I was the administrative governor, before I became chair, I came to understand how badly the Eccles Building really needed a serious renovation," Powell said, adding that the building is "not really safe" and not waterproof.
Trump has previously threatened legal action over the renovations and mocked the project’s cost and design.
"They’re building a basement into the Potomac River."},{"url":"https://www.npr.org/2026/01/11/nx-s1-5674034/fed-powell-subpoena-doj-building-renovation","title":"Federal Reserve receives DOJ subpoena in escalating pressure campaign : NPR","content":"Federal Reserve receives DOJ subpoena in escalating pressure campaign The Justice Department has subpoenaed the Fed over Chair Jerome Powell's testimony over the central bank's headquarters renovation.
The Justice Department has subpoenaed the Federal Reserve for information on the central bank's headquarters renovation, according to a video statement released by Fed Chairman Jerome Powell on Sunday.
Powell said the grand jury subpoenas, received by the Fed on Friday, threaten criminal indictments related to his testimony on the renovation project before the Senate Banking Committee last June.
But he suggested an ulterior motive in the Justice Department's probe."},{"url":"https://www.fox26houston.com/news/federal-reserve-chair-powell-under-criminal-investigation-over-hq-renovation-report","title":"Federal Reserve Chair Powell under criminal investigation over HQ renovation, report | FOX 26 Houston","content":"The renovation of the Fed’s two main office buildings in Washington’s Foggy Bottom neighborhood is estimated to cost $2.5 billion and is being funded by the central bank itself, not by taxpayers.
In June 2025, Powell told members of the Senate Banking Committee that "There’s no new marble.
The project is expected to be completed in the fall of 2027, with Washington-based employees expected to begin working in the building in March 2028."},{"url":"https://www.cnbc.com/2025/07/14/fed-chair-powell-asks-inspector-general-to-review-controversial-building-project.html","title":"Powell asks inspector general to review $2.5 billion renovation after Trump blasts Fed project","content":""We've got a real problem of oversight and excess spending," Kevin Hassett, director of the National Economic Council, said Monday on CNBC.
Fed Chair Jerome Powell asked for the review, following blistering criticism of the project, initially pegged at $2.5 billion but hit by cost overruns that have brought accusations from President Donald Trump and other administration officials of "fundamental mismanagement."
In separate comments, former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh, speaking Sunday on Fox News, called the renovation costs "outrageous" and said it was more evidence the central bank "has lost its way." Warsh is considered a strong contender to succeed Powell when the latter's term as chair expires in May 2026."},{"url":"https://www.cnn.com/2026/01/12/business/video/federal-reserve-criminal-investigation-jerome-powell-renovation-of-headquarters-dc-elisabeth-buchwald-digvid","title":"Federal prosecutors open investigation into Federal Reserve | CNN Business","content":"Federal prosecutors have opened a criminal investigation of Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell over his testimony about the central bank's $2.5 billion renovation of its headquarters in DC."}]}

The Trump administration this weekend ramped up its war with Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell by launching a federal investigation. **The big picture:** The investigation ostensibly concerns the central bank's $2.5 billion renovation project, but functions as an unprecedented escalation of the president's effort to influence the central bank over interest rates. **Driving the news:** The Department ofJustice is conducting a criminal investigation into Powell over his Congressional testimony on the building project, according to a report this weekend that the Fed confirmed. **What they're saying:** \"No one — certainly not the chair of the Federal Reserve — is above the law,\" Powell said in a video issued by the Fed. **Between the lines:** Trump's appointees are trying to lay out legal predicates to fire Powell for cause — specifically, that the Fed's renovation project has included changes not approved by a federal planning authority, and/or that Powell lied to Congress about the project, *Axios' Neil Irwin writes*.","score":0.75049835,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.nytimes.com/2026/01/12/us/politics/jerome-powell-investigation-fed-renovations.html","title":"What to Know About the Criminal Investigation of Fed Chair ...","content":"The probe revolves around Jerome H. Powell, who has led the central bank since 2018, and his oversight of a $2.5 billion renovation of the Fed's","score":0.73459625,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/business/economy/25-billion-renovation-center-dojs-criminal-investigation-federal-reser-rcna253540","title":"The $2.5 billion renovation at the center of the DOJ's criminal investigation of the Federal Reserve","content":"# The $2.5 billion renovation at the center of the DOJ's criminal investigation of the Federal Reserve. The historic renovation is more complex — and longer in the making — than the fight over how much it cost. Eccles Federal Reserve Board Building during renovation on July 24.Andrew Harnik / Pool via AP. When the Department of Justice served the Federal Reserve with subpoenas in a criminal investigation on Friday, it zeroed in on the years-long renovation of the central bank’s headquarters in Washington, D.C. But at issue here is not just the cost of the multibillion-dollar construction project. It's also the extent to which it has become a vehicle for the White House to apply political pressure on an institution designed to operate independently of the federal government. But while Trump has publicly pressed Powell for years over interest rates, it’s only in the last six months that the president has also been critical of the renovation’s multibillion-dollar price tag.","score":0.6244086,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-01-12/why-the-federal-reserve-s-renovation-costs-2-5-billion","title":"Why the Federal Reserve's Renovation Costs $2.5 Billion","content":"The cost of the work has ballooned to $2.5 billion, and allies of President Donald Trump have previously pressed for an investigation . The Federal Reserve's two offices have been under construction for three years. Renovations began around 2022 on two Federal Reserve offices, the Marriner S. Eccles Building and the Federal Reserve Bank of East Building, which were first assembled in the 1930s, according to plans reviewed by the National Capital Planning Commission in 2021. As questions swirl around the renovations, Business Insider gathered images of the construction, floor plans for the renovations, and design mock-ups. The Fed said the building has not had a modernization project in decades. The project was necessary to \"address a critical backlog of upgrades, to respond to changes in building codes and regulatory requirements, and to meet requirements for information technology, building security, environmental sustainability, and energy efficiency,\" according to plans produced by the Fed and released to the public in 2021.
